reading_prompt: What city in the United States has the largest population?
writing_sentence: New York City has the largest population in the United States.

reading_prompt: Who lived in America before the Europeans arrived?
writing_sentence: American Indians lived in the United States before the Europeans arrived.

reading_prompt: Who can vote in state and federal elections in the United States?
writing_sentence: Citizens can vote in state and federal elections.

reading_prompt: What was the war fought between the north and the south?
writing_sentence: The Civil War was fought between the north and the south.

reading_prompt: What are the first three words of the US Constitution?
writing_sentence: We the people are the first three words of the US Constitution.

reading_prompt: What are the names of three presidents who led the United States?
writing_sentence: Adams, Lincoln, and Washington are all presidents of the United States.

reading_prompt: Who lived in America before the country was founded?
writing_sentence: American Indians lived in the United States before the country was founded.

reading_prompt: When do people observe Columbus Day?
writing_sentence: Columbus Day is observed in October in many states.

reading_prompt: How many senators do we have in Congress in our country?
writing_sentence: The 50 states have 100 senators, two for each state.

reading_prompt: What are three states that are part of the United States of America?
writing_sentence: California, Alaska, and Delaware are three states in the United States.

reading_prompt: Which president is featured on the one-dollar bill?
writing_sentence: George Washington is featured on the one-dollar bill.

reading_prompt: What is the supreme law of the land in our nation?
writing_sentence: The United States Constitution is the supreme law of the land.

reading_prompt: What form of government does the United States have?
writing_sentence: The United States form of government is a republic.

reading_prompt: What are the two major political parties in the United States?
writing_sentence: The Democratic and Republican parties are the two major political parties.

reading_prompt: When must all men register for the Selective Service?
writing_sentence: All men must register for the Selective Service at age 18.

reading_prompt: What is one of the important responsibilities of US citizens?
writing_sentence: Voting in federal elections is an important responsibility of citizens.

reading_prompt: Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
writing_sentence: Thomas Jefferson wrote the Declaration of Independence.

reading_prompt: When was the United States Constitution written?
writing_sentence: The United States Constitution was written in 1787.

reading_prompt: What territory did the United States buy from France in 1803?
writing_sentence: The United States bought the Louisiana Territory from France in 1803.

reading_prompt: What ocean is on the West Coast of the United States?
writing_sentence: The Pacific Ocean is on the West Coast of the United States.

reading_prompt: What ocean is on the East Coast of the United States?
writing_sentence: The Atlantic Ocean is on the East Coast of the United States.

reading_prompt: What are two rights of everyone living in the United States?
writing_sentence: Freedom of speech and freedom of religion are two rights for everyone.

reading_prompt: What group of people was taken to America and sold as slaves?
writing_sentence: People from Africa were taken to America and sold as slaves.

reading_prompt: Why did the colonists fight the British in the Revolutionary War?
writing_sentence: The colonists fought the British because of taxation without representation.

reading_prompt: What did the Declaration of Independence do for the colonies?
writing_sentence: The Declaration of Independence announced our freedom from Great Britain.
